summary: PR 2538 advances decompilation of src/melee/if/ifprize.c by replacing stubs with recovered control flow and typed state for the ifprize flow. Bot output reports new 100% matches for fn_802FE470 and un_802FE6A8, near-complete improvements for un_802FE918 and un_802FEBE0_OnEnter, and a .data regression.

key_files.role: Only changed file; recovered ifprize state structures, event/state-machine behavior, object setup, text formatting, and matching scaffolding.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/if/ifprize.c added 157 deleted 67 hunks 8
raw_diff: entire diff for src/melee/if/ifprize.c
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for main/melee/if/ifprize
raw_diff: +#include "placeholder.h"

what_changed.text: Introduced and expanded typed local structs: un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 gained x0, x2, x4, and linked-list pointer x8; un_803F9D48 changed from padding/raw integers to HSD_JObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_Text, arg-list pointers, and an int x30[3] table.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added struct un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 before un_803F9D48
raw_diff: un_803F9D48 fields x10-x30 converted to HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_Text*, arg pointers, and int x30[3]

what_changed.text: Replaced pragma dont_inline around un_802FE3F8 with inline wrapper functions un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline, with a comment that un_802FEBE0_OnEnter would inline un_802FE3F8 otherwise.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop
raw_diff: added static inline un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline

what_changed.text: Implemented fn_802FE470 as a state-machine over un_803F9D48.x1, including prize display start, input handling, linked-list advancement via x2C->x8, cleanup of camera/light/UI gobjs, archive release, and animation updates.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802FE470 switch over un_803F9D48.x1 cases 0 through 3
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_802FE470 as a new 100.00% match

what_changed.text: Expanded un_802FE6A8 object setup to retain camera, light, UI gobj, jobj, and text handles in un_803F9D48; also narrowed object class constants with & 0xFF and scoped GXColor plus PAD_STACK inside a block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_802FE6A8 stores x18, x1C, x14, x10, x20, x24
raw_diff: HSD_GObj_804D784B & 0xFF and HSD_GObj_804D784A & 0xFF
raw_diff: block containing GXColor color and PAD_STACK(0x14)
text_corpus: bot report lists un_802FE6A8 as a new 100.00% match

what_changed.text: Expanded un_802FE918 from placeholder calls into prize audio selection, random x3 update, lookup through un_803F9B30/un_803F9A00, special handling for a == 0x3E, date/time formatting with language-dependent sprintf formats, and HSD_SisLib text output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_802FE918 body additions
text_corpus: bot report lists un_802FE918 improvement from 23.11% to 99.23%

what_changed.text: Adjusted un_802FEBE0_OnEnter to use the expanded argument struct, initialize x28/x2C/x3/x0b/x4, and call un_802FE3F8_noinline with casts to short pointers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_802FEBE0_OnEnter assignments to x28, x2C, x3, x0b, x4
raw_diff: un_802FE3F8_noinline(arg0x0, 2, (short*) &un_803F9D48.x6, NULL)
text_corpus: bot report lists un_802FEBE0_OnEnter improvement from 95.00% to 99.93%

smart_moves.text: Converted raw state storage in un_803F9D48 into concrete HSD object/text pointers and argument-list pointers, making later accesses more reviewable than anonymous padding and integer slots.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803F9D48 field rewrite

smart_moves.text: Kept text-section progress focused in one translation unit and used bot-reported function-level scores to show exactly which symbols matched or improved.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: one changed file
text_corpus: bot report with fn_802FE470, un_802FE6A8, un_802FE918, un_802FEBE0_OnEnter scores

smart_moves.text: Used ordinary C switch/for/if structure for the recovered state machine and table scans instead of leaving placeholder call sequences.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802FE470 switch cases
raw_diff: un_802FE918 for loops over k and un_803F9B30
raw_diff: un_802FEBE0_OnEnter for loop over un_803F9B30

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an unmatched function is mostly placeholder calls, recovering the surrounding persistent state struct can unlock multiple functions at once because setup, update, display, and teardown share the same object/text handles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803F9D48 gained HSD_JObj/HSD_GObj/HSD_Text fields
raw_diff: un_802FE6A8 stores handles later consumed by fn_802FE470 and un_802FE918
text_corpus: bot report shows two new matches and two near-complete improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: For state-machine callbacks, replacing flat placeholder calls with a switch on the persistent state byte can produce both readable source and strong matching progress.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_802FE470 switch on un_803F9D48.x1
text_corpus: fn_802FE470 reported as 100.00% after PR

decomp_lessons.lesson: Typed HSD handles and helper calls can express object ownership and cleanup directly: setup stores gobj/jobj/text handles, and teardown uses those stored handles for PLink/archive cleanup and animation calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_802FE6A8 stores x14/x18/x1C/x10/x20/x24
raw_diff: fn_802FE470 uses x18/x1C/x14 for HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 and x10 for DObj animation

naming_conventions.text: Most recovered globals and functions kept address-style names such as un_803F9D48, un_802FE918, and un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0; local variables use role names where clear, including gobj_camera, gobj_light, gobj_ui, jobj_ui, color, r, k, i, and sp1C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local declarations in un_802FE6A8 and un_802FE918
raw_diff: retained address-style function and struct names

naming_conventions.text: Temporary or inferred struct field names remain offset-style x0/x2/x4/x8/x10 etc., indicating semantics were not over-promoted beyond the evidence in this PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct un_802FEBE0_OnEnter_arg0 and struct un_803F9D48 field names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replaced a broad #pragma dont_inline block with a narrow call-wrapper tactic, un_802FE3F8_inner and un_802FE3F8_noinline, to affect inlining at the specific call site.

notes: Evidence is strong for changed file, diff content, symbols touched, and bot-reported match/regression scores.
PR body is empty and there are no loaded human review comments, so reviewer intent and author rationale are not available.
No local build log, objdiff transcript, or commit-by-commit rationale is loaded beyond the decomp-dev bot report.
The .data regression is reported by the bot and should not be ignored, but the slice does not explain whether it was accepted as a tradeoff or later resolved.
PAD_STACK and noinline wrapper tactics are visible in code; their necessity is plausible from comments and scores but not independently proven by detailed verifier output in this slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Persistent UI state structs can unlock multiple related ifprize functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2538, converting un_803F9D48 from padding/raw integer fields into HSD_JObj/HSD_GObj/HSD_Text and argument-list pointers connected setup, update, display, and cleanup logic. The same PR reported new matches for fn_802FE470 and un_802FE6A8 plus near-complete improvements for un_802FE918 and un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803F9D48 field rewrite
raw_diff: un_802FE6A8 stores handles used by fn_802FE470 and un_802FE918
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot score report

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer narrow inlining-control wrappers over broad pragma blocks when evidence supports the call-site issue
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2538 removed a #pragma dont_inline block around un_802FE3F8 and added narrow inline wrappers un_802FE3F8_inner/un_802FE3F8_noinline, with a local comment that un_802FEBE0_OnEnter would inline un_802FE3F8 otherwise. This should remain a tactic requiring verifier evidence, not a blanket style rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed pragma block
raw_diff: added wrapper functions and call from un_802FEBE0_OnEnter

source_update_candidates.title: Document wrapper-based inlining control as a narrow matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that wrapper functions used to steer MWCC inlining should be treated like other matching tactics: keep them narrow, comment the specific inlining problem, and attach objdiff/build evidence. PR 2538 provides an example where a broad #pragma dont_inline block was replaced by un_802FE3F8_inner/un_802FE3F8_noinline wrappers for un_802FEBE0_OnEnter.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_802FE3F8 pragma removal and wrapper addition
raw_diff: comment about un_802FEBE0_OnEnter inlining un_802FE3F8 otherwise
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already caution against pragmas and broad tactics, but this PR gives a concrete pattern for replacing a pragma with a narrower wrapper. Curator should decide whether this merits a source update.

source_update_candidates.title: Emphasize tracking data regressions when text progress lands
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2538 had clear text-section progress, including two new 100% function matches, while the bot also reported a .data regression from 96.59% to 93.59%. Consider reinforcing that text progress can land with data noise only when the regression is recorded and scoped for follow-up.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new function matches and .data regression
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover data regressions, but this PR is a compact example for curator consideration.

rejection_notes: Do not infer semantic names for un_803F9D48 fields or address-style functions from this slice; the PR kept offset/address-style names.
Do not promote the noinline wrapper pattern as generally preferred without requiring build/objdiff evidence; the slice only shows a local comment and bot score report.
Do not claim the .data regression was accepted, intentional, or harmless; the slice only reports its existence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale because no human review comments or review records are loaded.
